content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be repurposed to resolve a longstanding flaw in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning: the 'noisy TV problem', where intrinsically motivated agents get stuck repeating random, unpredictable stimuli instead of exploring generalizable knowledge. By routing curious exploration queries through an episodic memory system optimized to prioritize informative, under-learned state clusters (the core innovation of modern active learning memory), agents can automatically deprioritize noisy, unrewarding randomness while directing curiosity to high-value knowledge gaps.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
